skill.name: parallel-task
skill.description: Orchestrates parallel coding tasks from a markdown plan, using dependencies to launch subagents in ordered waves.
skill.summary: Run dependency-aware coding plans with parallel subagents.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by ordinary words such as description and describe. The Ruby backtick findings are Markdown formatting, but the skill does instruct agents to edit files, run validation, and create commits, so it should be published with clear user warnings.

medium_findings.title: Autonomous File Changes and Commits
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs subagents to implement changes, edit plan files, run validation, and commit work. This is expected for the skill, but it can modify repositories and execute project commands when used on untrusted plans. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The cited instructions explicitly direct file edits, validation, staging discipline, and commits; this is legitimate functionality but creates material operational risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited instructions explicitly direct file edits, validation, staging discipline, and commits. This is legitimate functionality but creates material operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Parallel Subagent Execution Amplifies Plan Risk
medium_findings.description: The skill launches all unblocked tasks in parallel based on content parsed from a plan file. A malicious or careless plan could cause multiple agents to make conflicting or unsafe repository changes at the same time. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The parallel launch behavior is directly documented, but exploitability depends on the plan content and available agent permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parallel launch behavior is directly documented. Exploitability depends on the plan content and available agent permissions.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Pattern Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are Markdown examples or fenced prompt templates, not executable Ruby code. The weak cryptography matches are ordinary English words such as description and describe, not cryptographic algorithm use. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The file is Markdown instruction text, and the cited lines do not contain code that invokes Ruby shell execution or weak cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown instruction text. The cited lines do not contain code that invokes Ruby shell execution or weak cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository-Modifying Agent Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to edit files, update plan documents, run validation, and commit completed work. Users should only run it on trusted plans and repositories where commits are expected. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The behavior is documented in the task prompt and validation workflow, but it is part of the declared purpose rather than hidden malicious logic.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is documented in the task prompt and validation workflow. It is part of the declared purpose rather than hidden malicious logic.

content.value_statement: Large implementation plans are slow when every task runs in sequence. This skill parses task dependencies and coordinates parallel subagents until the plan is complete.
actual_capabilities: Parses markdown plan files for task sections and dependency lists.
Filters execution to requested task IDs and required dependencies.
Launches unblocked tasks in parallel waves.
Gives subagents a structured prompt with goals, constraints, acceptance criteria, and validation.
Checks completed work and asks agents to retry or escalate failed tasks.
Maintains execution summaries with completed, blocked, and modified-file sections.
limitations: Requires a well-structured markdown plan with clear task IDs.
Does not verify task correctness beyond the available agent review and validation steps.
Can create repository changes and commits when the agent has those permissions.
May produce conflicts when plan dependencies are incomplete or inaccurate.

use_cases.title: Split Feature Builds
use_cases.description: Run independent feature tasks in parallel while respecting shared dependencies.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Refactor Plans
use_cases.description: Assign separate refactor steps to subagents and validate each wave before continuing.

use_cases.title: Manage Implementation Sprints
use_cases.description: Track task completion, issues, blockers, and changed files from a single execution summary.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with one plan file.

prompt_templates.title: Run Selected Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Execute only chosen task IDs and their required dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Resume Blocked Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Continue a plan after fixing blockers or reviewing earlier output.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Complex Build
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a detailed plan with dependencies, acceptance criteria, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Run a plan with four independent tasks and two dependent tasks.
output_examples.output: The skill assigns the four independent tasks first, validates their results, then launches the two dependent tasks.

output_examples.input: Run selected tasks T3 and T7 from a profile plan.
output_examples.output: The skill includes required dependencies, skips unrelated tasks, and reports completed work, blockers, and changed files.

output_examples.input: A subagent fails validation for one task.
output_examples.output: The skill records the issue, requests a retry or escalation, and does not advance dependent tasks until the problem is resolved.

best_practices: Use trusted plan files with explicit dependencies, acceptance criteria, and validation steps.
Review generated commits before merging or pushing changes.
Keep task scopes small enough for one subagent to complete and verify.
anti_patterns: Do not run this skill on untrusted plans from unknown sources.
Do not omit dependency links for tasks that touch the same files or interfaces.
Do not use it when repository commits are not desired.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It coordinates subagents that implement tasks from a markdown plan with dependency ordering.

faq.question: Does it run every task at once?
faq.answer: No. It launches only tasks whose dependencies are already complete.

faq.question: Can I run only part of a plan?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide specific task IDs, and the skill includes required dependencies.

faq.question: Will it change files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed for coding work and instructs agents to edit files and update the plan.

faq.question: Will it create commits?
faq.answer: Yes, when the agent has permission. The skill tells subagents to commit their own completed work.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted plans?
faq.answer: No. Use it only with plans you trust because the plan controls what subagents are asked to change.
